notes: International network of AI Safety Institutes. UK AISI established 2023, US AISI at NIST, Japan AISI, plus international coordination.

data.description: The first national AI safety institute, established after the Bletchley Summit in November 2023.
data.notes: Part of the UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology. Conducts pre-deployment safety testing of frontier AI models. Released the Inspect open-source AI evaluation framework.

data.description: US AI Safety Institute operating within NIST, established 2024.
data.notes: Part of NIST's AI program. Complements the AI Risk Management Framework. Develops evaluation methodologies and consensus standards for AI safety.

data.description: Japan's AI Safety Institute, focused on evaluating AI systems deployed in Japan.
data.notes: Established 2024. Works with METI (Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry). Partners with international AISIs on evaluation and standards.

match_nodes.description: International Network of AI Safety Institutes

data.official_name: International Network of AI Safety Institutes
data.description: Coordinating body for global AI safety efforts, formalized after the Bletchley Summit.
data.notes: Members include UK, US, Japan, Canada, France, Singapore, and EU. Coordinates shared evaluation methodologies, information sharing on risks, common terminology, and joint research.
